explanation.html_text:
Question / ప్రశ్న

Which of the following is NOT a feature of Project based learning?

కింది వాటిలో ప్రాజెక్ట్ ఆధారిత అభ్యసన లక్షణం కానిది

  1. Activeక్రియాశీలమైనది
  2. Traditionalసాంప్రదాయమైనది
  3. Experientialఅనుభవపూర్వకమైనది
  4. Inquiry basedవిచారణ ఆధారిత మైనది

Correct answer: B

Traditional

సాంప్రదాయమైనది

Why this is right

Project Based Learning (PBL) is a student-centred, constructivist approach where learners investigate real-world problems through active inquiry, collaboration, and hands-on experience. "Traditional" describes teacher-led, lecture-based, rote-memorisation instruction — the very opposite of PBL. The remaining options (Active, Experiential, Inquiry based) are all recognised hallmarks of PBL in the pedagogy literature, which is why they cannot be the NOT-feature. ప్రాజెక్ట్ ఆధారిత అభ్యసనం (PBL) విద్యార్థి కేంద్రీకృత పద్ధతి; సాంప్రదాయ బోధన దానికి వ్యతిరేకం.

Why the others are wrong

A – Active (క్రియాశీలమైనది): PBL requires students to actively do, build, investigate, and present — passivity has no place in it, so Active IS a feature.

C – Experiential (అనుభవపూర్వకమైనది): Learning by doing and reflecting on direct experience (Dewey's "learning by doing") is central to PBL, so Experiential IS a feature.

D – Inquiry based (విచారణ ఆధారిత మైనది): PBL always begins with a driving question or problem that students must investigate; inquiry is its backbone, so Inquiry based IS a feature.

Concept notes

Project Based Learning (PBL) is rooted in John Dewey's pragmatism ("learning by doing" — అనుభవం ద్వారా అభ్యసనం) and elaborated by William Heard Kilpatrick's Project Method (1918). Key features recognised in CTET/TET syllabi: (1) Active — learners are doers, not passive listeners; (2) Experiential — knowledge is constructed through real, meaningful experience; (3) Inquiry-based — a central driving question guides investigation; (4) Collaborative — students work in teams; (5) Reflective — structured reflection is built in; (6) Student-centred (విద్యార్థి కేంద్రీకృతం). Contrast this with Traditional (సాంప్రదాయ) instruction: teacher-directed, textbook-bound, lecture-heavy, convergent, and focused on rote recall (నిఘంటు కంఠస్థం). The NCF 2005 and NEP 2020 both explicitly advocate moving away from traditional methods toward experiential and inquiry-based approaches — making this distinction exam-relevant. Remember: PBL = progressive; Traditional = antithesis of PBL.

Likely spin-off TET questions

  1. Which theorist is most associated with the "Project Method" in education? (Answer: William Heard Kilpatrick)
  2. Which of the following is a feature of Project Based Learning? (A) Rote memorisation (B) Teacher-centred instruction (C) Collaborative inquiry (D) Passive listening) — Answer: C
  3. NCF 2005 recommends shifting away from which type of learning toward experiential approaches? (Answer: Traditional / rote-based learning)

Memory hook

"PBL = AIE — Active, Inquiry, Experiential" — if an option breaks this triplet (like "Traditional"), it is the odd one out. సాంప్రదాయం PBL కాదు; AIE మాత్రమే PBL.
